notes: Extracted from the 'MU' sheet's linearity table formulas. Unlike Pressure/Temperature/Electrical, this is a procedure with intermediate dependent state, not a flat list of independent coefficients — see type_a and type_b_sources below for how each component is actually derived. The eccentric-loading formula in the source file applies a /2*SQRT(3) step immediately followed by /SQRT(3), which mathematically cancels to just /2 — reproduced faithfully below since that's what the lab's real spreadsheet does; flagged to confirm with Charkha whether that's intentional.

type_a.description: MAX(stdev_near_zero, stdev_fifty_percent, stdev_hundred_percent) / SQRT(10). The single worst of the three repeatability tests, divided by sqrt(n_readings), applied uniformly to every linearity table point rather than computed per-point.

type_b_sources.name: u_std_weights
type_b_sources.description: Uncertainty of standard (UM) — combined cert uncertainty of the standard weight combination used at this load point

type_b_sources.name: u_eccentric
type_b_sources.description: Eccentric/off-center loading (UL). Source file formula is (max_minus_min/2 * SQRT(3)) / SQRT(3), which cancels to max_minus_min/2 — implemented as the cancelled form.

cmc_source: cmc_bands lookup by load value, instrument_type='Weighing'

cmc_table_from_source_file.note: This was the worked example in the source file for a specific 100kg scale. Confirm with Charkha whether these are the lab's actual accredited CMC values or specific to that one example instrument before seeding cmc_bands.
